{{Quote|Nada Synnt (oh, ''please'', Trooper …) has been keeping his operatives away, but this must have changed with Tion's death, since Danken and a couple of others are definitely drifting in that direction.|[[Cynabar's InfoNet]]|<ref name="GNN3" />}}
'''Nada Synnt''' was a common alias used by a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who was in charge of a [[Nada Synnt's organization|group of operatives]]. He was sometimes mentioned in [[Cynabar's InfoNet]] reports. Synnt liked to use punny aliases, including "Tosin Dice" and "Benner Dunnit."

==Biography==
The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] known as '''Nada Synnt''' led an [[Nada Synnt's organization|organization]] that operated during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. "Nada Synnt" was one of many aliases adopted by the smuggler, but it was the most prominent and widely used, and he was famous enough in smuggling circles to be mentioned regularly in updates from the [[Cynabar's InfoNet]] [[NewsNet]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Tion (Lord)/Legends|Tion]] on [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]], Synnt authorized his operatives to start smuggling goods through the [[blockade of Ralltiir]]. He had previously kept his organization away from Ralltiir.<ref name="GNN3" />

Several months later, Synnt was still operating under the "Nada Synnt" alias, which Cynabar considered to be a record. By that point, he had lost his main supplier of [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] components, but had acquired a new [[ropaji]] dealer and was starting to encroach on [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] operations.<ref name="GNN5">{{JournalCite|5|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>

A [[Standard year/Legends|year]] later, Synnt had finally shed the name, and was operating under the aliases "Tosin Dise" and "Benner Dunnit". He was also flying a new [[Starship/Legends|Starship]], a [[Corellian Desslin]] named the ''[[Pareesh D'Thot]]'', which he used to help the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] "misplace" [[List of phrases and slang/Legends#R|rawmat]] shipments.<ref name="GGN10">{{JournalCite|10|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>

Nada Synnt's name was later used by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Tinian I'att Azur-Jamin|Tinian I'att]] and [[Chenlambec]] to fool [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]] into thinking [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was present on [[Lomabu III]].<ref name="Prize Pelt">{{StoryCite|story=The Prize Pelt: The Tale of Bossk|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}</ref>

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
Nada Synnt's true name is not revealed, but in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]], Cynabar refers to him by the designation "Trooper", which may be a reference to [[Alexsandr Badure|Alexsandr "Trooper" Badure]]. However, no source had positively equated the two characters.
